hero.question: Will Connecticut win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: Connecticut at 14.6% is the crowd saying "not happening", you're getting paid like a long shot despite a legitimate tournament team with NBA talent in the Big East. Dan Hurley's roster is tournament-caliber most years, but 14.6% underprices variance and home-court magic in March, especially in a region where UConn draws a crowd advantage.

daily_take.headline: Connecticut's Tournament Odds Stuck in Neutral While Arizona Surges
daily_take.deck: Connecticut flatlined at 14.6% despite decent recruiting momentum, while Arizona jumped 2.2 points to 35.4% on stronger roster construction talk. The gap between these two programs keeps widening, but Connecticut's price hasn't adjusted. Smart money should examine whether Connecticut's rebuilding timeline gets priced in or if the market is sleeping on their trajectory relative to Arizona's current hype.

sidebar.headline: Arizona's 35.4% implies a legitimate title contender, yet recruiting class comparisons suggest Connecticut could offer better value at half the price.
sidebar.label: Arizona Tournament Win: 35.4%
